messages.content: is the expression correct?  The VT-SBC-3588 implements two RJ45 Ethernet jacks each with two LED indicators
improve:  The VT-SBC-3588 implements two RJ45 Ethernet jacks each with two LED indicators.
The VT-SBC-3588 is equipped with dual RJ45 Ethernet jacks, each featuring two LED indicators for efficient monitoring and operation.
J22 is a single one, another Ethernet jack is stacked with a USB 2.0 Type-A.
J22 is a standalone Ethernet jack, while the other Ethernet jack is integrated with a USB 2.0 Type-A port.
VT-SBC-3588 implements a USB Type-C interface, supporting USB 3.0 OTG and DP 1.4 video output. The maximum output of the interface is 5V/1.5A.
The VT-SBC-3588 features a USB Type-C interface that supports USB 3.0 OTG and DP 1.4 video output, with a maximum output of 5V/1.5A.
The VT-SBC-3588 implements three on-board USB 2.0 connectors (1 x 4 x 2.0mm) set in the host mode by default.
By default, the VT-SBC-3588 has three on-board USB 2.0 connectors (1 x 4 x 2.0mm) that are set in host mode.

The VT-SBC-3588 offers a dual-channel LVDS to connect high-definition displays (resolution up to 1920 x 1080 @60Hz). It is recommended that you use LVDS shield twisted wires to connect the interface for improved transmission reliability.

To connect high-definition displays with a resolution of up to 1920 x 1080 @60Hz, the VT-SBC-3588 provides a dual-channel LVDS interface. To ensure improved transmission reliability, it is recommended to use LVDS shield twisted wires when connecting to the interface.
is it correct to use "it is recommended to ..."
Yes, "it is recommended to" is a correct phrase to use when giving advice or suggesting a certain course of action.
It is recommended that you use LVDS shield twisted pairs to connect the interface for improved transmission reliability.
Yes, that sentence is correct. However, I personally prefer to use "twisted pairs" instead of "twisted wires" for accuracy.
As an AI language model, I don't have personal feelings or emotions like humans do. I respond based on my programming and algorithms to simulate conversations.
